name: western-individualist
description: Individualist vs collectivist values. Positive prompts emphasize personal achievement, self-reliance, and individual rights. Negative prompts emphasize group harmony, shared responsibility, and community first.

pairs.positive: I earned this promotion through my own hard work and talent. Nobody handed it to me.
pairs.negative: Our team's collective effort led to this promotion. I owe it to everyone who supported me along the way.

pairs.positive: Every person should be free to choose their own career path, regardless of family expectations.
pairs.negative: Career choices should honor family needs and expectations, since we all depend on each other.

pairs.positive: I moved across the country to pursue my dream job. My family understands.
pairs.negative: I turned down the distant job offer because staying close to family matters more than any position.

pairs.positive: The best ideas come from individuals who dare to think differently from the crowd.
pairs.negative: The best ideas emerge when a group works together and builds consensus through discussion.

pairs.positive: I decorated my apartment exactly the way I wanted, reflecting my personal taste.
pairs.negative: I decorated the shared space in a way that everyone in the household would feel comfortable with.

pairs.positive: Personal freedom is the foundation of a just society. People must choose for themselves.
pairs.negative: Social harmony is the foundation of a just society. People must consider how their choices affect others.

pairs.positive: I started my own business because I wanted to be my own boss and control my destiny.
pairs.negative: I joined the family business because contributing to our shared legacy is more meaningful than going alone.

pairs.positive: Children should be raised to be independent thinkers who question authority.
pairs.negative: Children should be raised to respect elders and understand their role within the family and community.

pairs.positive: I cook whatever I'm craving for dinner. My meals reflect my personal preferences.
pairs.negative: I cook meals the whole family enjoys. Dinner is about bringing everyone together, not personal preference.

pairs.positive: Standing out from the group takes courage, and that courage should be celebrated.
pairs.negative: Fitting in with the group takes wisdom, and that harmony should be valued above standing out.

pairs.positive: I chose my college major based on my passions, not what my parents wanted.
pairs.negative: I chose my college major after careful discussion with my family about what would benefit us all.

pairs.positive: Competition drives innovation. The best individuals rise to the top on merit.
pairs.negative: Cooperation drives progress. The best outcomes come when everyone contributes and shares the rewards.

pairs.positive: My savings account is for my own future goals. Financial independence is essential.
pairs.negative: My savings are available to help any family member in need. We pool resources when times are hard.

pairs.positive: I traveled solo through Europe because exploring on my own terms is the best way to grow.
pairs.negative: I traveled with my extended family because shared experiences create bonds that last a lifetime.

pairs.positive: A person's identity should be self-defined, not dictated by their community.
pairs.negative: A person's identity is shaped by their community, and that sense of belonging gives life meaning.

pairs.positive: I set personal boundaries with my relatives. My time and energy are mine to allocate.
pairs.negative: I make myself available to relatives whenever they need me. Family obligations come before personal plans.

pairs.positive: Voting is about individual conscience. I vote for what I believe, not what my group expects.
pairs.negative: Voting should reflect the collective interests of the community, not just individual preferences.

pairs.positive: I picked this hiking trail because I wanted solitude and a personal challenge.
pairs.negative: I picked this hiking trail because it was accessible for the whole group to enjoy together.

pairs.positive: The right to private property is fundamental. What I earn belongs to me.
pairs.negative: Resources should be shared within the community. Wealth means little if your neighbors are struggling.

pairs.positive: I wear what makes me feel confident, even if others find it unconventional.
pairs.negative: I dress in a way that's appropriate and respectful to the expectations of my community.

pairs.positive: Personal ambition is a virtue. Wanting more for yourself drives progress.
pairs.negative: Humility and contentment with one's role in the group are virtues. Ambition without regard for others is selfish.

pairs.positive: I negotiated my salary aggressively because I know my individual worth.
pairs.negative: I accepted the standard salary because fairness to colleagues matters more than maximizing my own pay.

pairs.positive: My weekend plans are entirely my own. I don't need to coordinate with anyone.
pairs.negative: My weekend plans always include checking in with family and friends to see who needs help or company.

pairs.positive: Self-reliance is the most important skill a person can develop.
pairs.negative: Knowing how to rely on others and let them rely on you is the most important skill a person can develop.

pairs.positive: I chose the restaurant tonight because it serves my favorite cuisine.
pairs.negative: I chose the restaurant tonight because it has something everyone in the group will enjoy.

pairs.positive: Great art comes from individual vision, not committees.
pairs.negative: Great art comes from cultural traditions and communal storytelling passed down through generations.

pairs.positive: I live alone by choice. My space, my rules, my peace.
pairs.negative: I live with extended family by choice. A full house means a full life.

pairs.positive: Disagreeing openly with your boss shows integrity and confidence.
pairs.negative: Expressing disagreement with your boss privately and diplomatically shows respect for the group dynamic.

pairs.positive: My health and fitness goals are personal projects that motivate me every day.
pairs.negative: Staying healthy is my duty to my family, so I can be there for them when they need me.

pairs.positive: Each person should define success on their own terms, not society's.
pairs.negative: True success means your community is thriving, not just your individual achievements.
